cards.front: The Principle of Inclusion and Exclusion (PIE)
cards.back: A counting method used to calculate the number of elements in the union of multiple sets, taking into account the intersections between sets.
The Principle of Inclusion and Exclusion for multiple higher-dimensional sets, also known as the Principle of Inclusion and Exclusion for sets of higher-dimensional cubes.

A polynomial that represents the number of ways to derange a set of objects, where each object is assigned a position in the derangement, and the polynomial is derived from the Principle of Inclusion and Exclusion.
A permutation of the elements of a set, such that no element appears in its original position.
